2604.06650.title: A Parameter-Efficient Transfer Learning Approach through Multitask Prompt Distillation and Decomposition for Clinical NLP
2604.06650.paper_summary: Existing prompt-based fine-tuning methods typically learn task-specific prompts independently, imposing significant computing and storage overhead at scale when deploying multiple clinical natural language processing (NLP) systems. We present a multitask prompt distillation and decomposition framework that learns a single shared metaprompt from 21 diverse clinical source tasks and adapts it to unseen target tasks with fewer than 0.05% trainable parameters. Evaluated across five clinical NLP task types (named entity recognition, relation extraction, question answering, natural language inference, and summarization) on 10 held-out target datasets using three backbone models (LLaMA 3.1 8B, Meditron3 8B, gpt-oss 20B), our framework consistently outperforms LoRA by 1.5~1.7% despite using orders of magnitude fewer parameters, and exceeds single-task prompt tuning by 6.1~6.6%. The gpt-oss 20B model achieves the highest overall performance, particularly on clinical reasoning tasks. The strong zero- and few-shot performance demonstrates better transferability of the shared prompt representation.
2604.06650.paper_summary_zh: 現有的基於提示的微調方法通常獨立學習特定任務的提示，這在部署多個臨床自然語言處理（NLP）系統時會帶來顯著的計算和存儲開銷。我們提出了一個多任務提示蒸餾和分解框架，該框架從21個多樣的臨床源任務中學習一個單一的共享元提示，並將其適應於未見的目標任務，訓練可調參數少於0.05%。在使用三個主幹模型（LLaMA 3.1 8B、Meditron3 8B、gpt-oss 20B）對10個保留的目標數據集進行的五種臨床NLP任務類型（命名實體識別、關係提取、問題回答、自然語言推理和摘要）的評估中，我們的框架在參數數量少了數個量級的情況下，始終比LoRA高出1.5~1.7%，並超過單任務提示調整6.1~6.6%。gpt-oss 20B模型在整體性能上達到最高，特別是在臨床推理任務上。強大的零樣本和少樣本性能顯示出共享提示表示的更好可轉移性。
2604.06650.author: Cheng Peng et.al.
2604.06650.authors: Cheng Peng, Mengxian Lyu, Ziyi Chen, Yonghui Wu
